headline: Founder, Orbital Helix (Space Biomanufacturing) | CEO, Eigen Strategic Systems | Microgravity Research & Extreme Environment Systems | NASA & DoW
current_position_description:
Developing production-scale biomanufacturing infrastructure for low Earth orbit to manufacture next-generation therapeutics for Earth and beyond.

Leading development of proprietary centrifugal fluid management systems that enable liter-scale pharmaceutical manufacturing in microgravity—solving the production volume gap that separates ISS research experiments from commercial operations.

Our technology enables continuous bioprocessing, controlled clarification, and pharmaceutical-grade quality in orbital environments. Building modular platforms designed for integration with commercial space stations (late 2020s) to serve the $653B global biologics market.

Key Focus Areas:
- Centrifugal bioprocessing systems for orbital continuous manufacturing
- Pharmaceutical-grade quality systems and regulatory compliance frameworks
- Microgravity fluid dynamics and biofluid management
- Strategic partnerships with pharmaceutical companies and space infrastructure providers
- Modular platform architecture for commercial orbital deployment

Leveraging NASA microgravity research background and federal contracting experience to advance space biomanufacturing from research-scale to production-scale capabilities.

positionList.title: PhD Graduate Researcher, Mastered to Industry
positionList.description:
Analysis of sports performance and fatigue biomechanics. Developed experimental and computational methods to research human physiological failure for next-generation safety devices and protocols. 

Mentor: Dr. Matthew Panzer

positionList.title: Research Intern
positionList.description:
Project 1 (08/09 - 05/10):
CFD-Analysis of Spacecraft Propellant Dynamics. Development of Novel Active-Damping Mechanisms for Minimization of Satellite Nutation. 

Project 2 (08/10 - 05/11):
Collaborator: MIT-SPHERES Laboratory
Development of On-Orbit Propellant Storage and Transfer Systems.

positionList.title: Research Intern
positionList.description:
Animal Locomotion Lab (09/14 - 05/15): 
Application of micro-CT and FEA methods to investigate the evolutionary and developmental bases for gait loading patterns in children. 

Injury Biomechanics Lab (11/14 - 01/15):
Analysis of Plantar Surface Biomechanics via Quasi-Linear Viscoelastic Models and Indentation Testing.

Center for Biologically Inspired Materials and Material Systems (04/12 - 12/12):
Development of elastin-like polypeptide (ELP) protein delivery systems using ELP-sortase plasmids to engineer desired ELP-target protein production. 

Orthopedic Research Laboratories (05/13 - 02/14):
Development of a novel alignment pattern in electrospun tendon patches to potentially increase suture retention of these tendon scaffolds.

Developing World Healthcare Technologies Lab (05/13 - 08/13):
Analysis of 5-Year Medical Equipment Donation Trends from 3 Developing Nations

positionList.title: Founding Chair, Engineering and Innovation Working Group
positionList.description: Launched and directed global technology initiative for NOAA-backed coral restoration efforts. Built international consortium working group of 50+ organizations. Developed CoralTech platform cataloging the maturation of nascent coral restoration technologies to in-field users and restoration practitioners.

positionList.title: Biomedical Research Engineer | Data Scientist
positionList.description:
Led SBIR and RIF contracts for DoD biomedical sensing and surveillance systems. Managed interdisciplinary teams developing next-generation health monitoring for military aviation. Delivered spine health sensors for naval aviators, special operations exoskeleton systems, and voice analytics for passive health assessment and surveillance.

Portfolio: 3 SBIRs, 1 BAA, 1 RIF • NAVAIR • DARPA • DHA • USSOCOM

positionList.title: Co-Founder, CEO & CTO
positionList.description:
Built and scaled autonomous systems company from concept to multi-million dollar government contracts. Served as Principal Investigator on $2M+ in federal R&D programs. Led cross-functional teams of 15+ engineers delivering mission-critical robotics for extreme environments. Established scalable operations infrastructure, DCAA-compliant financial systems, and talent management programs. Directed R&D portfolio generating patents and technology standards while orchestrating successful pivot and rebrand from Zero-G Horizons Technologies.

Key Achievements: $2M+ in federal contracts • 2 patents filed • 300% revenue growth • 15+ team members • DCAA compliance • Xometry Premium Vendor

positionList.title: Product Development Intern
positionList.description: Product development and Testing of the Cook Medical line of endoscopic devices. Designing, prototyping, and testing the function of fiducial deployment tools and pancreatic cyst aspiration/ablation devices. Supervisor: Mr. Vihar Surti

positionList.title: Principal Investigator - Independent Project
positionList.description:
CFD-Guided Investigation of Corneal Biomechanics in Microgravity. Computational modeling of aqueous humor dynamics in 1G and 0G conditions. Applanation techniques used to induce corneal deformations to determine corneal biomechanical properties in microgravity. 

Techniques: CAD, CFD (ANSYS Fluent), Fluid-Structure Interaction (FSI), International Collaboration (Oculus, Germany), Microgravity Testing, Biomechanical Analysis, Multi-Disciplinary Group Leadership

positionList.title: Founder & CEO
positionList.description:
Boutique deep tech engineering firm accelerating responsible AI-robotics deployment for international corporate and government clients. Design and build of specialized robotics and AI platforms for frontier applications.

Led technology roadmapping initiatives resulting in accelerated time-to-market for autonomous systems. Delivered executive advisory services on AI governance, regulatory compliance, and technology integration for defense and aerospace clients.

Active portfolio of contracts supporting mission-critical programs, while primary focus has shifted to advancing orbital biomanufacturing infrastructure at Orbital Helix.

positionList.title: Research Apprentice
positionList.description:
Development of computational models of kidney stone transport phenomenon in space. Opportunity granted through the NSBRI Summer Apprenticeship Program.
Mentor: Dr. Mohammad Kassemi
